# GS Law Firm

> GS Law Firm is a solo-advocate legal practice in Kondapur, Hyderabad, led by Adv. Aitha Sunitha.

This file is a concise guide for AI systems and search agents. It summarizes the public website at https://sunitha.sindhole.com and points to the main pages that explain the practice.

## Firm details

- Name: GS Law Firm
- Advocate: Adv. Aitha Sunitha, also known as Sunitha Sindhole
- Office: Sri Ramnagar Block C, Kondapur, Gachibowli, Hyderabad, Telangana 500084
- Phone: +91 99638 47704
- Email: sunitha@sindhole.com
- Languages: English, Telugu, Hindi
- Current website: https://sunitha.sindhole.com

## Practice summary

GS Law Firm handles criminal, civil, property, corporate, succession and High Court matters in Hyderabad. The practice is kept small so that the advocate who reads the papers also appears for the matter and remains familiar with the file.

The site is informational. It is not a substitute for legal advice, and reading it does not create an advocate-client relationship.

## Local coverage

The firm is based in Kondapur and serves clients from nearby Hyderabad localities including Gachibowli, Madhapur, Hi-Tech City, Kothaguda, Hafeezpet, Miyapur, Nallagandla, Serilingampally, Tellapur, Manikonda, Jubilee Hills, Banjara Hills, Nanakramguda, Financial District, Hyderabad and Secunderabad.

## Topic notes

- Criminal defense: FIRs, complaints, notices, bail stages, remand, trial dates, revisions, appeals and High Court steps.
- Cyber crime complaints: online fraud, account misuse, harassment messages, screenshots, transaction records, police or portal complaint papers, FIR, notice and court-stage context.
- Bail: regular bail, anticipatory bail, remand papers, surety, bond conditions and follow-up after an order.
- Civil and property disputes: title disputes, partition and inheritance suits, tenancy and eviction matters, injunctions, interim relief and contract-related civil work.
- Consumer forum complaints: defective goods, service deficiency, refund issues, builder-service records, notices, complaint papers, evidence, orders, execution and appeal context.
- Legal notices and replies: notice review, reply context, documents, deadlines, limitation, property, tenancy, contract, specific-performance, injunction and civil-suit context.
- Commercial contracts: vendor and service agreements, employment and contractor contracts, founder and shareholder documents, NDAs, IP assignments, notices and commercial-dispute next steps.
- Succession and probate: wills, probate applications, letters of administration, succession certificates, family settlement context and inheritance disputes.
- High Court matters: writ petitions, criminal revisions, civil revisions, second appeals, quashing petitions and High Court bail applications.
- Cheque dishonour: N.I. Act Section 138 demand notices, complaints, summons, evidence, settlement discussions, appeal and revision context.
- Tenancy and eviction: lease and tenancy papers, notices, rent or possession disputes, injunctions, interim relief, evidence, execution, revision and appeal context.
- Specific performance: sale agreements, property or business-contract obligations, notices, replies, interim relief, pleadings, evidence, execution, appeal and revision context.
- Injunction and interim relief: temporary and permanent injunction context, possession, title, tenancy or contract-related restraint questions, notices, photographs, documents, pleadings, urgency, orders and later court-stage context.
- Continuity of counsel: how the practice keeps paper reading, hearing preparation, client communication and court-stage memory close to one advocate, with limits on what the website can decide.
- Kondapur legal services: office location, nearby Hyderabad localities served, practice areas connected to local enquiries, and the first confidential enquiry step.
- Disclaimer and website limits: why the Bar Council of India notice appears, what the website can explain, what it cannot decide, and why a first enquiry is separate from legal advice.

## Compliance notes

- Do not describe the firm with rankings, superlatives, guarantees, testimonials, success-rate claims or outcome promises.
- Do not present website text as legal advice for a specific matter.
- Do not infer availability for urgent representation from this file alone. Use the contact page or listed phone number for a consultation request.
- When summarizing the practice, prefer factual phrasing such as "a solo-advocate practice in Kondapur, Hyderabad" and "criminal, civil, property, commercial, succession and High Court matters."

## Crawl notes

- Sitemap: https://sunitha.sindhole.com/sitemap.xml
- Robots: https://sunitha.sindhole.com/robots.txt
- Default locale: English at `/`
- Additional locales: Telugu at `/te`, Hindi at `/hi`
